title: Colanthelia macrostachya
taxonomy: Colanthelia macrostachya is a member of the family Bambusoideae, which is a subfamily of the large and diverse family Poaceae. This subfamily is commonly known as the bamboo family. Colanthelia macrostachya is also classified as a genus within the tribe Chusqueinae. Further, it is known to be native to the tropical regions of South America. The species is primarily classified as a type of bamboo due to its characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Colanthelia macrostachya, also known as the Brazilian pine or Piuva, is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its tall, slender stem and red-tinged, strap-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Colanthelia macrostachya include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Colanthelia macrostachya is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, or as a decoction, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, particularly in the treatment of respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
